rationale: The cited paper appears only as part of grouped example citations when the authors note that prior marketing research has examined product-related inferences (e.g., alongside Hamilton et al. 2014, Hair and Ozcan 2018, He and Bond 2013, and Moore 2015). The citing paper does not discuss Kivetz and Simonson (2000)'s incomplete-information mechanism, findings, or design, and it is not used to build specific hypotheses, measures, or analyses. Its role is therefore broad background rather than a distinct, functional input to the profanity model.
